# LLMify AG

> Sovereign AI infrastructure for Swiss companies. Open-source models on own infrastructure, without dependency on US cloud providers.

LLMify builds AI solutions where data never leaves the company. From strategy to custom AI applications — on Swiss infrastructure, on-premise or sovereign cloud.

## Services

- **AI Strategy**: Use case analysis, prioritisation, and roadmap development
- **AI Infrastructure**: Open-source models on Swiss infrastructure (on-premise or sovereign Swiss cloud)
- **AI Applications**: Custom applications with intelligent document search and agent workflows

## Insights

- [Nemotron 3 Super](https://llmify.ch/insights/nemotron-3-super/): NVIDIA releases an open-weight model competing with Qwen 3.5 at 7.5x higher throughput. The on-premise choice becomes strategic.
- [US Export Controls on AI](https://llmify.ch/insights/us-exportkontrollen-ki/): The US "Remote Access Security Act" could regulate cloud access to AI. Models on own infrastructure are unaffected.
- [Qwen 3.5 Release](https://llmify.ch/insights/qwen-35-release/): Qwen 3.5 brings native vision and stronger reasoning on the same hardware. Open source is closing the gap faster than expected.
- [Which Benchmarks Matter for Swiss SMEs](https://llmify.ch/insights/benchmarks-schweizer-kmu/): GPT-5.2 leads in PhD math, but in general knowledge across 14 languages the lead is just 2.5%. For SME work, the difference is negligible.
- [EU Parliament Shuts Down AI](https://llmify.ch/insights/eu-parlament-ki/): The EU Parliament disables AI on all devices — IT cannot guarantee where data flows. The answer is AI on infrastructure you control.
- [The ICC Incident](https://llmify.ch/insights/icc-vorfall/): The International Criminal Court loses access to its Microsoft email and switches to open source. Digital dependency is operational risk.
- [CLOUD Act](https://llmify.ch/insights/cloud-act/): The US CLOUD Act requires American companies to hand over data — even from servers in Switzerland. Data location is not data sovereignty.
